Title: Shen
Age: Current31 – 36 years old Timeline Born961 AN – 966 AN Edit
Occupation: Primary Leader of the Kinkou Order

children.title: Early life
children.textContent:
Shen was born to Great Master Kusho, the Eye of Twilight, one of the three leaders of the Kinkou Order. As the son of the Great Master, he immersed himself in the order's culture, and its core tenets were as familiar to him as the Ionian sunset. He knew the necessity of Pruning the Tree, the determination of Coursing the Sun, but above all, he learned the wisdom of Watching the Stars. He meditated and studied throughout his childhood, and was considered exemplary by all his teachers.

One evening, Master Kusho took pity on a masterless servant boy, Govos, and invited him to spar with his son. Though Shen easily won each bout, the boy continued to rise and demand rematches. Watching closely, Kusho offered guidance on how to counter Shen's strikes. Seeing Govos's determination and knowing his father's ways, Shen finally allowed the boy to land a hit on him. Impressed, Kusho took Govos under his wing. Shen and Govos trained together thereafter, growing as close as brothers.

children.title: Hunt for the Golden Demon
children.textContent:
When the Council of Zhyun sent an envoy to Great Master Kusho begging for aid in stopping the infamous killer known as the Golden Demon, Kusho agreed, taking Govos and Shen with him, and began a covert investigation. Together, they tended to families shattered by the killings, dissected the horrific crime scenes, and looked for connections between the murders.

During this time, Kusho had arranged for Shen to marry Yevnai. As they made their way to Kotha Villega, Shen and Govos talked about his enagement. Though Shen was excited, he was also nervous, but Govos reassured him that Yevnai loved him as well. When they arrived at the village, they found it massacred by the demon. They tried to tend to the survivors, and Govos worked desperately to save as many lives as he could, but the scale of the carnage shook him to his core. When they were finished, Kusho tried to comfort Govos. Impressed by his resolve, Kusho named Govos his successor as the Eye of Twilight and granted him a new name, Usan.

Their investigation spanned four long years, leaving all three men changed. Shen, once known for his wit and humor, grew increasingly somber; Usan, the brightest star of Kusho's temple, began to struggle with his studies; and Kusho's famous red mane turned white. When Kusho finally discovered a pattern in the killings, he realized they were not hunting a demon, but a wicked man or vastaya. Believing the matter to be beyond the Kinkou's mandate, he sought to hand off the investigation. However, Shen and Usan refused to turn back after all they had endured, and convinced him to continue.

Three years into the investigation, on the eve of the Spirit Blossom Festival in Jyom Pass, Kusho disguised himself as a renowned calligrapher to blend in among the visiting artists, while Shen and Usan carefully prepared a trap. Usan allowed himself to be captured by the Golden Demon, and using his necklace, Kusho and Shen tracked him to his location. As the demon prepared for his performance, Shen burst through the wall to save Usan. Usan seized one of the killer's blades and attempted to strike him down, but Kusho intervened, insisting they take the killer back alive.

In the aftermath, Kusho was proven right, and the so-called demon was revealed to be a human stagehand named Khada Jhin. Kusho decided that Jhin should be imprisoned within the monastery prison at Tuula. While both Shen and Usan believed Jhin deserved a far harsher punishment, Shen accepted his father's judgment and attempted to comfort a bitter and resentful Usan.

children.title: Rise of the Order of Shadow
children.textContent:
When Noxus began its invasion of the First Lands, Masters Kennen, Kusho, and Mayym ruled to not involve the Kinkou in the war. Shen tried to adopt his father's dispassion and appeared to support their decision, but secretly began sneaking out to train soldiers and attack enemy supply lines. Usan helped cover for him by deliberately throwing his matches, making it appear as though Shen was keeping up with his training. However, Usan had grown disillusioned with the Kinkou and left the Order after they failed to retaliate against Noxus.

As the war raged on, Shen focused on maintaining Ionia's spiritual harmony. When he sensed an imbalance within the Order, Shen rushed back to their temple and was met by the survivors of a bloody coup. Usan-now calling himself Zed-had returned with a small band of warriors and usurped the Kinkou's temple, killing many of the Order, including his father. Zed and his followers now wielded dangerous shadow magic and called themselves the Order of Shadow. Repressing his anguish, Shen led the remaining Kinkou to safety in the mountains. He took up his father's spirit blade and assumed the mantle of the Eye of Twilight, vowing to rebuild the Order. Following the core tenets, Shen began recruiting and training new members in hopes of restoring the Kinkou's strength. As Shen struggled to lead the fractured Kinkou, Kennen helped guide the Order.

After his father was killed, Shen's engagement to Yevnai was called off.

Several months after Master Kusho's death, the Kinkou retreated to an abandoned temple east of Thanjuul. On the final day of the Spirit Blossom Festival, Shen met with Kennen and Mayym to decide the Order's response to the war. Mayym argued that Ionia's recent victory at the Placidium justified taking action against those who disrupted the balance, including both Noxians and Ionians. However, Kennen believed the situation to be more complicated, warning they should choose their battles carefully. When they looked to Shen for guidance, he agreed that Ionians also inflamed the war, notably Zed and his order. However, he believed his judgment was compromised when it came to matters regarding his former brother, choosing to withdraw until he could decide impartially.

Not long afer, Shen sensed a disturbance in the spirit realm and alerted Kennen and Mayym. They rushed off to a nearby valley and found Kinkou neophytes being attacked by a corrupted spirit. They quickly dispatched the creature's physical form and Shen entered the spirit realm to deal with it there.

When Mayym's daughter Akali came of age at fourteen, she began her Kinkou training under Shen's tutelage. Her mother had stood by his father as the Fist of Shadow, and it appeared that Akali would follow the same path. While she showed boundless potential, Shen often found himself urging retraint when Akali faced mortal opponents.

children.title: End of the Invasion
children.textContent:
After the Noxian invasion finally ended, many Ionians celebrated their hard-won victory, but Shen bore the lasting consequences of war. He remained steadfast in his duty, yet in private he wrestled with his hatred for Zed and doubted his ability to lead. The years of conflict had taken a heavy toll on the First Lands, and Shen was uncertain whether the rebuilt Kinkou could ever restore the balance. Zed and his order reached out to the Kinkou, and though he did not reconcile with Shen, they came to an uneasy accord.

When the Temple of Twilight suddenly returned to the material, Kennen rushed off too confer with Shen. However, neither he nor Mayym could reach it in time to stop Atakhan from breaching the Temple's defenses, leading Yunara and Xin Zhao to undergo the Trials of Twilight to stop it.

Shen went to aid a fisherman who reported that his ship had been attacked by a demon. As Shen took the man's account, a holy man and his acolytes arrived, declaring that they would defeat the spirit themselves. The holy man asked if Shen would aid them, and Shen affirmed that balance would be restored. Shen tracked the demon to its resting place and drew his spirit blade, ready to strike. However, he soon heard the cries of dying spirits and abandoned his quarry to investigate. Teleporting back to the shipwreck, Shen found the holy man and his acolytes destroying innocent spirits. When the holy man offered him the next kill, Shen refused. He sheathed his spirit blade and drew his steel sword instead. Shen apologized to the holy man and leveled his blade against them, intent on restoring balance.

Akali eventually proved herself worthy and was named the new Fist of Shadow. However, Akali's spirit was restless and believed Shen insistence on restraint held her back. She began to argue with and disobey Shen, and before long left the Order. Shen did not stop her, knowing this was a path Akali needed to walk alone. With the Fist of Shadow unfilled, only Kennen and Shen remained of the Kinkou triumvirate.

Despite leaving the Order, Akali still aided the Kinkou. She accompanied Shen to Navori Province, where they encountered a young Noxian woodsman cutting down a guardian tree. Intent on teaching him a lesson, Akali destroyed his cart, trapping him beneath it. As she confronted the boy, the tree's sap awakened its guardian, furious at the Noxian. Shen shared his ability to peer into the spirit realm with Akali, revealing the source of the guardian's rage. Despite his warnings, Akali rushed to attack the spirit, but was unable to stop it. Shen insisted that balance had to be restored, but Akali pleaded with him to bend the rules to spare the child. As Akali tried to free the boy, she realized what Shen meant and plucked a nearby seed. Using her kunai, she dug a small trench and instructed the boy to bury it. As the spirit prepared to strike, Akali used one of her charms to water the soil, causing the seed to bloom. Appeased, the guardian stayed its hand. Shen then nurtured the growing tree further, and the spirit settled beneath its roots, returning once more to its slumber.

children.title: Working with Zed
children.textContent:
Two days after the attack on the port, Shen had Zed restrained and prepared for transport when he received a message from Akali. Shen confronted Zed and told him that he knew he had been holding back and that he allowed himself to be captured. Zed questioned if Shen had discovered who had helped Jhin escape, but Shen admitted that he had not and promised to protect Zed if Jhin came for him. Alternatively, Shen offered Zed one final chance to work together to stop Jhin on the condition that he finally tells him the truth about why he had killed his father.

Zed claimed that he needed the power of the Tears of Shadow to stop the Noxians, and that Master Kusho had tried to stop him. Shen saw through his lie, pointing out that Zed could have taken the Tears without killing Kusho. Zed then confessed that he had been jealous of how much Kusho loved Shen and believed he needed shadow magic to become stronger. Again, Shen saw through his lie, as Kusho loved him just as much, and demanded the truth. At last, Zed admitted that Kusho had been right: if the Kinkou became involved in the war, their sacred mission would be corrupted. To save Ionia, a new order had to be born, but if Zed had merely taken the Tears, members of the Kinkou would have followed him. To sever that possibility, he had to become the Kinkou's hated enemy and betray Master Kusho. Shen accepted Zed's answer and freed him. While Shen wanted to capture Jhin, Zed insisted that he had to die. Shen refused, arguing that Jhin did not threaten the balance, but Zed countered he was not a member of the Kinkou. However, Shen refused to let Zed die thinking their father had been wrong and if he wanted his help, this was the price.

As they prepared to depart, Shen informed Zed that Jhin had escaped to Piltover, and that Akali had gone after him. Upon arriving in the City of Progress, they attempted to follow her trail. Zed again pressed Shen on whether he had discovered who had helped Jhin escape, but Shen dismissed it, as the only ones who knew where Jhin was being held were the two of them, Yevnai, and his father. One of Shen's informants eventually guided them an old opera house where Akali had been last spotted. There, they encountered an agent of the Navori Brotherhood who revealed he had led her into Jhin's trap. Shen and Zed then rushed in to save her.

They found Akali on the far side of a bridge, bound to one of Jhin's props and guarded by his traps, while Jhin sat nearby, calmly observing. Shen teleported in to strike him down, but it was only a decoy, and he was caught in a barrage of explosives. The real Jhin then revealed himself and moved to execute Akali, but Zed emerged from the shadows and the two rushed him together. Jhin evaded their attacks and unleashed more traps, incapacitating them both, though Zed managed to free Akali.

As Jhin prepared to finish Shen off, Akali struck, pinning Jhin to the ground and raising his own gun to kill him. Shen pleaded with her to stop, but Zed knocked her aside and demanded to know how Jhin had escaped. Jhin replied that he had not escaped but, rather, he was freed, and that Zed already knew by whom. Enraged, Zed hurled Jhin at Shen's feet and declared their bargain fulfilled before vanishing into the shadows.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Shen is a tall, muscular man with fair skin and brown hair. His hair is typically tied back with the sides shaved. His eyes glow white-blue whenever he utilizes his magic.

His signature armor is a full-body ninja regalia in pristine condition. It is dark blue with a silver trim, covering all but his eyes and upper arms. Though he carries a traditional blade on his back, he can also summon a spirit blade composed of ethereal violet magic.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Known for his dispassion, Shen is calm and even-keeled, possessing the aura of esoteric wisdom that befits his station. Extreme events, however, can cause him to become aggressive and emotional, especially in the presence of his despised rivals such as Zed or Jhin. Still, Shen fights honorably, striving to protect the innocent and preserve the balance of Ionia.

children.title: Kinkou Order
children.textContent: Shen leads the surviving Kinkou which has a great conflict of interest with Zed's Order of Shadow and its defiance of Ionia's sacred balance. Kennen remains by his side while Akali went rogue. She and Shen parted on neutral terms. Neither hates the other but both disapprove of the other's methods. After Akali and Zed made their choice, Shen is starting to doubt whether the old way still works.

children.title: Zed
children.textContent: Shen and Zed are childhood friends and used to consider each other as brother before Zed "murdered" Master Kusho. Master Kusho, Shen, and Zed were responsible for capturing and imprisoning Khada Jhin, but were traumatised by the Golden Demon's actions in the process. Later on, when Jhin was set free again, Zed sought out Shen in order to inform him about Jhin's reappearance and to team up with him to stop him. Shen, despite still wishing to avenge his father's death, accepted the truce for the greater good. The duo later saved a captured Akali from Jhin and caught him. Shen is torn between killing Zed to avenge his father Kusho (the Kinkou's previous master and wielder of the spirit blade) and enforcing equilibrium with the spirit realm (his duty takes priority, but he won't hesitate if given the chance). Zed could have revealed the truth about Kusho to Shen but then decided it was better for Shen to be disillusioned with Zed than to be disillusioned with his own father. That's probably not fair, and maybe Shen has a right to know. But Zed is flawed... and blinded by his need to feel he's worthy and honorable.

children.title: Yevnai
children.textContent: Shen was arranged to be married to Yevnai but the engagement was called off after Zed "killed" Master Kusho. Zed and Yevnai were at one point secretly in a relationship even when forbidden by Master Kusho, unknown to Shen though he possibly suspected it.

mainContent.textContent:
